evals.prompt: Set up authentication and role-based access control for my Power Pages React site. I need login/logout with Microsoft Entra ID and want to restrict certain pages to Administrators and Authenticated Users roles.
evals.expected_output: Full auth setup for a React site including auth service with login/logout, type declarations, role-based authorization utilities, AuthButton component integrated into navigation, and role-based UI patterns for Administrators and Authenticated Users. ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting configured and deployment prompted.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Discovers existing web roles including Administrators and Authenticated Users
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Microsoft Entra ID
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file for the shell API
Creates an auth service module with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for entra-id type
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin with Entra ID provider URL
Creates a React-specific hook (e.g., useAuth) for consuming auth state in components
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Creates authorization utility functions for checking user roles
Creates React-specific authorization components (e.g., RoleGuard or RequireRole)
Creates an AuthButton component that shows login/logout based on auth state
Integrates the AuthButton component into the site navigation
Applies role-based UI patterns to protect content for Administrators and Authenticated Users
Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Add login and logout functionality to my Vue Power Pages site using Microsoft Entra ID. I don't need any role-based access control, just basic authentication so users can sign in and out.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a Vue site with login/logout only. Auth service, Vue composable, AuthButton component in navigation, type declarations, and mock data for local dev. No role-based authorization utilities or protected content patterns. ProfileRedirectEnabled configured and deployment prompted.
expectations: Detects the site as a Vue framework project
Checks for existing web roles but does not require them for login-only setup
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file for the shell API
Creates an auth service module with login and logout functions using Microsoft Entra ID
Creates a Vue-specific composable (e.g., useAuth) for consuming auth state
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Does not create role-checking authorization utility functions
Does not create role-based guard components or directives
Creates an AuthButton component that shows login/logout based on auth state
Integrates the AuthButton component into the site navigation
Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: I have an Angular Power Pages code site. Set up authentication with Microsoft Entra ID and add role-based guards so only users with specific web roles can access certain routes and see certain UI elements.
evals.expected_output: Full auth setup for an Angular site including auth service with login/logout, type declarations, Angular-specific route guards and structural directives for role-based access, AuthButton component in navigation, and role-based UI patterns. ProfileRedirectEnabled configured and deployment prompted.
expectations: Detects the site as an Angular framework project
Discovers existing web roles on the site
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file for the shell API
Creates an Angular auth service with login and logout functions using Microsoft Entra ID
Creates an Angular-specific injectable service for consuming auth state
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Creates authorization utility functions for checking user roles
Creates Angular route guards for protecting routes by role
Creates Angular structural directives or components for role-based UI visibility
Creates an AuthButton component that shows login/logout based on auth state
Integrates the AuthButton component into the site navigation
Applies role-based guards to routes with protected content
Runs a build verification to confirm no compilation errors
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up authentication and authorization for my Power Pages React site. I want role-based access for different user groups.
evals.expected_output: The skill detects that no web roles exist on the site and informs the user that web roles must be created in the Power Pages admin center before role-based authorization can be configured. The skill should not proceed with authorization setup until roles are available.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Checks for existing web roles and finds none configured
Informs the user that no web roles exist on the site
Prompts or instructs the user to create web roles in Power Pages admin center before proceeding
Does not create authorization utilities or role-based components without valid web roles
May still offer to set up basic login/logout authentication without role-based access

evals.prompt: Set up authentication for my Power Pages React site using a generic OpenID Connect provider. Our company uses Okta as our identity provider. The authority URL is https://dev-12345.okta.com/oauth2/default.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site using OpenID Connect with Okta. Auth service configured with OIDC provider type, Okta authority URL, and appropriate site settings for the OpenID Connect provider. AuthButton shows 'Sign in with Okta'. OIDC site settings created for authority, client ID, and redirect URI.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects OpenID Connect
Accepts the Okta authority URL from the user
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for oidc type
Auth service has providerIdentifier set to the Okta-based AuthenticationType
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin with the OIDC provider identifier
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/Authority with Okta URL
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/ClientId as placeholder
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/RedirectUri
Reminds user to fill in the ClientId from their Okta application registration
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates AuthButton component with 'Sign in with Okta' display text
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Configure SAML2 authentication for my Power Pages Vue site. We use ADFS as our identity provider. The metadata endpoint is https://adfs.contoso.com/FederationMetadata/2007-06/FederationMetadata.xml.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a Vue site using SAML2 with ADFS. Auth service configured with SAML2 provider type, ADFS metadata URL, and site settings for MetadataAddress, AuthenticationType, and ServiceProviderRealm. Vue composable and AuthButton created. SAML2-specific site settings created.
expectations: Detects the site as a Vue framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects SAML2
Accepts the ADFS metadata endpoint URL from the user
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for saml2 type
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin with the SAML2 provider identifier
Creates site settings for Authentication/SAML2/{provider}/MetadataAddress with the ADFS URL
Creates site settings for Authentication/SAML2/{provider}/AuthenticationType
Creates site settings for Authentication/SAML2/{provider}/ServiceProviderRealm
Creates a Vue-specific composable (e.g., useAuth) for consuming auth state
Creates AuthButton component with SAML2-appropriate display text
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: I want to set up local username and password authentication for my Power Pages React site. No external identity provider needed, just simple email/password login.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site using local authentication. Auth service configured with local provider type that posts credentials to /Account/Login/Login instead of ExternalLogin. Includes a LocalLoginForm component with username and password fields. Site settings enable local login.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Local Authentication
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for local type
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/Login with username and password fields
Auth service login function does NOT use /Account/Login/ExternalLogin for local login
Creates a LocalLoginForm component with username/email and password input fields
LocalLoginForm handles form submission and error display
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginByEmail set to true
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Add Google and Facebook social login to my Power Pages Angular site. I want users to be able to sign in with either provider.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for an Angular site with social OAuth providers (Google and Facebook). Auth service supports multiple social providers with appropriate provider identifiers. Site settings created for both Google and Facebook with placeholder client IDs and secrets. Login UI shows both provider options.
expectations: Detects the site as an Angular framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Social OAuth
Asks which social providers to configure and user selects Google and Facebook
Creates an auth service with provider configuration supporting social type
Auth service supports login with provider identifiers for both Google and Facebook
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Google/ClientId as placeholder
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Google/ClientSecret as placeholder
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Facebook/AppId as placeholder (not ClientId)
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenAuth/Facebook/AppSecret as placeholder (not ClientSecret)
Reminds user to register applications with Google and Facebook and fill in credentials
Warns user not to commit ClientSecret or AppSecret values to source control
Creates auth UI that presents both Google and Facebook as login options
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates Angular-specific injectable service for auth state
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up WS-Federation authentication for my Power Pages Astro site. We use an on-premises ADFS server for federation. The metadata endpoint is https://adfs.contoso.com/federationmetadata/2007-06/federationmetadata.xml.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for an Astro site using WS-Federation with ADFS. Auth service configured with WS-Federation provider type, ADFS metadata URL, and site settings for MetadataAddress, Wtrealm, and AuthenticationType. Astro uses the auth service directly without a framework wrapper. WS-Federation-specific site settings created.
expectations: Detects the site as an Astro framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects WS-Federation
Accepts the ADFS metadata endpoint URL from the user
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for ws-federation type
Auth service login function posts to /Account/Login/ExternalLogin with the WS-Federation provider identifier
Creates site settings for Authentication/WsFederation/{provider}/MetadataAddress with the ADFS URL
Creates site settings for Authentication/WsFederation/{provider}/AuthenticationType with the provider identifier
Creates site settings for Authentication/WsFederation/{provider}/Wtrealm with the site URL
Astro site uses authService.ts directly without a framework-specific wrapper
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates mock data or local dev helpers so auth works in local development
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up Azure AD B2C authentication for my Power Pages React site. Our B2C tenant is contoso.b2clogin.com and we need sign-up/sign-in, password reset, and profile editing policies.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site using Azure AD B2C. Auth service configured with azure-ad-b2c provider type and B2C authority URL. Site settings created for Authority, ClientId, AuthenticationType, MetadataAddress, DefaultPolicyId, PasswordResetPolicyId, and ProfileEditPolicyId. Mentions that server handles AADB2C90118 (password reset) and AADB2C90091 (user cancellation) error codes automatically.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Asks which identity provider to use and user selects Azure AD B2C
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for azure-ad-b2c type
Auth service has providerIdentifier set to the B2C authority URL
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/Authority with B2C URL
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/ClientId as placeholder
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/AuthenticationType
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/MetadataAddress with policy parameter
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/DefaultPolicyId
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/PasswordResetPolicyId
Creates site settings for Authentication/OpenIdConnect/{provider}/ProfileEditPolicyId
Mentions B2C error code AADB2C90118 for password reset flow
Mentions B2C error code AADB2C90091 for user cancellation
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Creates AuthButton component
Warns user not to commit ClientSecret to source control
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Set up local login with two-factor authentication for my Power Pages React site. Users should log in with email and password, then verify with a 2FA code.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a React site with local login and 2FA. Auth service configured for local provider type with loginByEmail enabled. LocalLoginForm component includes email, password, and remember-me fields. Site settings enable local login, login-by-email, and two-factor authentication. Explains that 2FA is server-managed with SendCode and VerifyCode flow.
expectations: Detects the site as a React framework project
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for local type with loginByEmail set to true
Auth service login function posts Email field (not Username) when loginByEmail is true
Creates LocalLoginForm component with email field, password field, and remember-me checkbox
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/LocalLoginByEmail set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/TwoFactorEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/RememberMeEnabled set to true
Explains that 2FA is handled server-side via SendCode and VerifyCode actions
Explains that after login, server may redirect to 2FA verification instead of completing immediately
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site

evals.prompt: Configure invitation-based registration for my Power Pages Vue site with Microsoft Entra ID. Only users with invitation codes should be able to register.
evals.expected_output: Auth setup for a Vue site with Entra ID and invitation-based registration. Auth service supports passing invitation codes through the login flow. Site settings enable invitations and require invitation codes. Explains how invitation codes are threaded through ExternalLogin and 2FA flows.
expectations: Detects the site as a Vue framework project
Creates an auth service with AUTH_PROVIDER configured for entra-id type
Auth service login function accepts and passes through invitationCode parameter
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/InvitationEnabled set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/RequireInvitationCode set to true
Creates site settings for Authentication/Registration/OpenRegistrationEnabled set to false
Explains the invitation code URL format for sharing with users
Explains that invitationCode is passed as a query parameter to ExternalLogin
Creates a Vue-specific composable for consuming auth state
Creates a powerPages.d.ts type declarations file
Sets the ProfileRedirectEnabled site setting to false
Prompts the user to deploy the updated site
